RecapTitle: St. Andrew's defeats Cathedral Academy in region opener
Recap:
St. Andrew's defeated Cathedral Academy 4-1 in a SCISA Region 2-AA boys soccer game on March 22, 2004. Will Massey scored once, Hamish Huntley scored twice, and Beau Hinton added a goal.

Massey and Hinton were credited with assists, and Emmett Eason made two saves as St. Andrew's improved to 1-0 overall and 1-0 in SCISA Region 2-AA play.

RecapTitle: St. Andrew's shuts out Charleston Collegiate in region play
Recap:
St. Andrew's defeated Charleston Collegiate 13-0 in a SCISA Region 2-AA boys soccer game on March 23, 2004. Beau Hinton, Omar Glenn, and Hamish Huntley scored two goals apiece, while Danny Eichholz, Will Massey, Jacob Rauers, Freddy Presutto, Graham Rossell, and Martin Sullivan also scored. The published scoring line accounts for 12 of St. Andrew's 13 goals.

Glenn was credited with three assists, Massey with two, and Eichholz, Rauers, and Sullivan with one each. St. Andrew's improved to 2-0 overall and 2-0 in SCISA Region 2-AA play, while Charleston Collegiate moved to 1-3.

Jacob Rauers was credited with an assist, and Emmett Eason made six saves as St. Andrew's improved to 3-0 overall and 3-0 in SCISA Region 2-AA play. Beaufort Academy moved to 3-3.

RecapTitle: St. Andrew's shuts out St. Francis Xavier in state quarterfinal
Recap:
St. Andrew's defeated St. Francis Xavier 9-0 in a SCISA Class AA state tournament quarterfinal on May 10, 2004. Will Massey, Jacob Rauers, Hamish Huntley, Omar Glenn, Beau Hinton, Daniel Eichholz, Will Chisholm, Kevin Crouch, and Remy Thurston scored for St. Andrew's.

Massey was credited with two assists, while Huntley, Eichholz, Chris Rauers, Ben Harrison, Matt Knight, and Joe Bilton each added one assist. Emmett Eason and Patrick Maleki made two saves apiece. The brief listed St. Andrew's at 10-1-1.

Notes: The Savannah Morning News recap listed St. Andrew's at 12-1-1 after this state semifinal, indicating at least one additional unreported win in the recovered schedule. The article noted St. Andrew's would play Calhoun Academy or James Island in the state final at 4:30 p.m. Saturday at Heathwood Hall in Columbia, S.C.
RecapTitle: Saints advance to SCISA Class AA/A championship
Recap:
SAINTS ADVANCE TO SCISA CLASS AA/A CHAMPIONSHIP - ST. ANDREW'S, WITH A 12-1-1 RECORD, WILL PLAY SATURDAY FOR ITS SECOND STRAIGHT TITLE AND FIFTH IN THE LAST EIGHT YEARS.

St. Andrew's entered its SCISA Class AA/A soccer semifinal match Wednesday as just another school in Savannah with a decent soccer team.

Following their 7-1 victory over Charleston's Cathedral Academy, the Saints (12-1-1) gave notice they may be as good as anyone else in town, and will play Saturday for their second straight title and fifth in the last eight years.

St. Andrew's will take on Calhoun Academy or James Island in the state final at 4:30 p.m. Saturday at Heathwood Hall in Columbia, S.C.

The Saints opened the scoring early when senior midfielder Will Massey put a shot over the Generals' defense that sailed under the crossbar by mere inches. Massey had two goals and an assist to lead the Saints' offense.

"Our guys played brilliant soccer today," said Saints coach Derek Johnson. "They went out and gave it the best effort of the season, and I believe we'll carry this over into the championship match Saturday."

To say the Saints were dominant would be an understatement. They outshot the Generals 16-4 in the first half, and did not allow a shot in the second half while taking 19 of their own.

"I think we did well with what we had," said Cathedral coach Brian Price. "We're a very young team, and we just played one of the most experienced teams in the state, one that should win the championship."

The only score for the Generals came when a direct kick by Jacob Chinchinsky was deflected back into the box by Saints goalkeeper Emmett Eason, who had three saves, and Kenneth Chandler knocked the loose ball into the net.

"This is the best we've played all year," Saints' soccer coordinator Mark Gibbons said. These kids have a knack for winning the big game, and this was a demonstration of that. We played these guys twice this year, and they gave us fits. So coming out and beating them like this, in the state semifinals, was a big win for us."

Hamish Huntley, the leading scorer for the Saints, had two goals within a two-minute period of the first half. The first came off an assist by Massey when he lofted the ball into the box and Huntley directed the ball into the goal with a jumping header. The second came off an assist by Andy Crouch, who made a pass in traffic that Huntley smashed into the corner of the net.

Senior midfielder Beau Hinton controlled the game in the middle for the Saints. His persistence pursuit of the ball paid off when he hit a shot from the top of the box with 7:51 left that gave the Saints a 5-1 lead.

Daniel Eichholz closed out the scoring for the Saints when he made a run from the right side into the box and beat Generals' goalkeeper Sean Monahan for the final 7-1 margin.

Goals-CA-Kenneth Chandler; SA-Will Massey 2, Hamish Huntley 2, Jacob Rauers, Beau Hinton, Daniel Eichholz. Assists-SA, Massey, Andy Crouch. Saves-CA, Sean Monahan 10; SA-Emmett Eason 3. Records-CA 10-9, SAA 12-1-1.

Notes: St. Andrew's won its second straight SCISA Class A/AA boys soccer championship at Heathwood Hall School in Columbia, S.C.
RecapTitle: Saints win second straight SCISA Class A/AA soccer title
Recap:
SAINTS WIN SECOND STRAIGHT SCISA CLASS A/AA SOCCER TITLE

St. Andrew's won its second straight SCISA Class A/AA boys' soccer championship Saturday, steamrolling James Island (S.C.) Christian 5-1 at Heathwood Hall School.

Daniel Eichholz scored two first-half goals and had two assists for the Saints. Omar Glenn scored 30 seconds into the second half to help catapult St. Andrew's to the victory.

"This is as good a team as I've ever coached," said St. Andrew's coach Mark Gibbons, who has coached at public schools Gordon Central and East Paulding, along with private school Darlington. "The last two games we've been dominant. The thing with us is we can put 11 kids on the field that can play the game."

At Heathwood Hall, Columbia, S.C.

Boys

St. Andrew's 5, James Island Christian 1

Goals-JIC, Caleb Costa; SA, Daniel Eichholz 2, Omar Glenn, Jacob Rauers, Hamish Huntley. Assists-Huntley 2, Eichholz 2. Saves-SA, Emmett Eason 6. Records-SA 13-1-1.
